questions.question: Who is the main character of the story?
questions.explanation: John Dolittle is the kind doctor who eventually becomes a doctor for animals.

questions.question: What kind of doctor was John Dolittle at the start of the story?
questions.explanation: He initially treated human patients in the town of Puddleby-on-the-Marsh.

questions.question: What is the name of Dr. Dolittle's parrot?
questions.explanation: Polynesia is the wise parrot who teaches the doctor how to talk to animals.

questions.question: Who first suggests that Dolittle should become an animal doctor?
questions.explanation: The Cat's-food-Man noticed Dolittle's love for animals and thought he would be a better vet than a human doctor.

questions.question: Why did human patients stop coming to see Dr. Dolittle?

options.B: They didn't like the animals and the smell in his house
options.C: He moved to a different city

questions.explanation: People were uncomfortable sharing the waiting room with hedgehogs and other pets.

questions.question: Polynesia tells the Doctor that animals talk using their _____.

options.B: Sounds and body parts like ears and tails

questions.explanation: Animals use a combination of vocal sounds and physical signals to communicate.

questions.question: What was the 'A.B.C.' of the birds that Polynesia taught the Doctor?

options.B: The basic sounds and meanings of bird language

questions.explanation: Polynesia started with the basics of bird communication so the Doctor could understand them.

questions.question: Which animal is the Doctor's loyal dog?
questions.explanation: Jip is the faithful dog who stays by the Doctor's side.

questions.question: Dab-Dab is the Doctor's pet _____.
questions.explanation: Dab-Dab is the duck who often helps take care of the house.

questions.question: What problem did the plough horse have?

options.B: He was going blind and needed glasses
options.D: He wanted to be a racehorse

questions.explanation: The horse's eyes were weak, and other vets hadn't understood what he needed.

questions.question: What did Dr. Dolittle give the plough horse to help him see?
questions.explanation: The Doctor fitted him with large green glasses that made him see much better.

questions.question: Where does Dr. Dolittle live?
questions.explanation: Puddleby is the small, fictional town where the story begins.

questions.question: Who is Sarah Dolittle?
questions.explanation: Sarah is his sister who looks after the house and often complains about the animals.

questions.question: What special thing did the Doctor build for the mice?

options.B: A separate door or tunnel into the house

questions.explanation: He made sure every animal had a safe way to enter his clinic.

questions.question: How did animals in other countries hear about Dr. Dolittle?

options.B: Birds told them during their winter flights
options.D: They read about it in a newspaper

questions.explanation: Migrating birds spread the word to animals all over the world.

questions.question: What animal is Gub-Gub?
questions.explanation: Gub-Gub is the Doctor's pet pig who loves food.

questions.question: What is Chee-Chee's animal species?
questions.explanation: Chee-Chee is the monkey the Doctor rescues from an organ-grinder.

questions.question: Why is Polynesia able to teach the Doctor animal language?

options.B: She can speak both bird-talk and people-talk

questions.explanation: Parrots have the unique ability to bridge the gap between human and animal speech.

questions.question: How does Dr. Dolittle feel about money compared to animals?

options.B: He cares more about animals than becoming rich
options.C: He thinks animals should pay him in gold
options.D: He doesn't know what money is

questions.explanation: Despite becoming poor, he is happy because he is helping creatures he loves.

questions.question: What was 'Puddleby-on-the-Marsh'?

options.A: The name of the Doctor's boat
options.B: The town where the Doctor lived

questions.explanation: It is the setting of the story.

questions.question: What did the old ladies bring to the Doctor?
questions.explanation: Once word got out, people brought their animals to him for treatment.

questions.question: Which quality best describes Dr. Dolittle?
questions.explanation: His kindness toward all living things is his most important trait.

questions.question: Polynesia told the Doctor that animals have 'quiet' ways of talking, like using their _____.
questions.explanation: Polynesia mentioned twitching noses and ears as ways animals speak.

questions.question: What happened to the Doctor's practice as a people's doctor?

options.B: It failed because people stayed away
options.C: He gave it to his sister
options.D: He closed it to go on vacation

questions.explanation: His house was so full of animals that his human patients stopped visiting.

questions.question: Ultimately, the story teaches us to _____.

options.A: Respect and be kind to all living beings

questions.explanation: The central theme is empathy and compassion for animals.
